questions.question: What is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: The Nile River is traditionally considered the longest river in the world, stretching over 6,600 kilometers.
questions.hostLeadIn: Let’s start with a classic debate.
questions.explanationStyled: The Nile usually takes the title at about 6,600 km. The Amazon might argue, but for now, Nile still gets the crown.

questions.question: Which country has the largest population in the world as of 2023?
questions.explanation: India surpassed China in 2023 to become the most populous country in the world.
questions.hostLeadIn: Population counts… and one very recent shift.
questions.explanationStyled: India took the top spot in 2023, edging past China. Billions of people, and somehow still counting.

questions.question: Which mountain range separates Europe from Asia in Russia?
questions.explanation: The Ural Mountains are commonly considered the natural boundary between Europe and Asia in Russia.
questions.hostLeadIn: Where continents quietly draw the line.
questions.explanationStyled: The Ural Mountains. Not the most dramatic range, but historically they’ve been the official Europe–Asia divider.

questions.question: Who was the first President of the United States?
questions.explanation: George Washington served as the first President from 1789 to 1797.
questions.explanationStyled: George Washington. The original president. Set the tone, then stepped away before things got messy.

questions.question: The Magna Carta, signed in 1215, limited the powers of which English monarch?
questions.explanation: King John was forced by his barons to sign the Magna Carta, limiting royal authority.
questions.explanationStyled: King John. The barons essentially told him to sit down and sign, and history’s been referencing it ever since.

questions.question: Which war was fought between the North and South regions of the United States from 1861 to 1865?
questions.explanation: The American Civil War was fought over issues including slavery and states' rights.
questions.hostLeadIn: One of the most defining conflicts in U.S. history.
questions.explanationStyled: The Civil War. Four years that reshaped the country and settled some questions the hard way.

questions.question: What planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.explanation: Mars appears red due to iron oxide, or rust, on its surface.
questions.hostLeadIn: A little astronomy, nice and easy.
questions.explanationStyled: Mars. Covered in iron oxide, which is just a fancy way of saying the whole planet looks rusted.

questions.question: What is the chemical formula for water?
questions.explanation: Water is made of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.
questions.explanationStyled: H2O. Two hydrogen, one oxygen. Simple, essential, and doing a lot of heavy lifting for life.

questions.question: Which scientist proposed the theory of general relativity?
questions.explanation: Albert Einstein introduced general relativity in 1915, describing gravity as the curvature of spacetime.
questions.hostLeadIn: Time to bend space a little.
questions.explanationStyled: Albert Einstein. He redefined gravity as spacetime curvature and made everyone else’s physics look outdated overnight.

questions.question: Which movie features the quote, "I'll be back"?
questions.explanation: Arnold Schwarzenegger delivers the iconic line in The Terminator (1984).
questions.hostLeadIn: One of the most quoted lines ever.
questions.explanationStyled: The Terminator. Short line, massive impact. Arnold didn’t need more words.

questions.question: Which TV series follows the lives of employees at the Dunder Mifflin paper company?
questions.explanation: The Office is a mockumentary-style sitcom centered on a paper company in Scranton, Pennsylvania.
questions.explanationStyled: The Office. A paper company somehow became one of the most quoted shows of all time.

questions.question: Who played the character of Jack Dawson in the 1997 film "Titanic"?
questions.explanation: Leonardo DiCaprio starred as Jack Dawson in Titanic.
questions.hostLeadIn: Back to one of the biggest films ever made.
questions.explanationStyled: Leonardo DiCaprio. King of the world, briefly, before things went very badly.
